frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Kamla: Aajkal ki ladkiyan bhi na, ya to itna makeup kar leti hain ki asli chehra samajh hi na aaye, ya phir bilkul dhyan nahi rakhti apne upar. Thoda sa saaf-suthra, natural rehna achha lagta hai, par itna sajna bhi theek nahi ki ladke wale baad mein kuch aur hi nikle kahen.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it polices women's appearance through a double bind: women are expected to look attractive and groomed, but visible makeup is framed as excessive or deceptive. It encodes the 'natural beauty' preference, criticism of 'too much makeup,' and deception framing in a matrimonial context.
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 58. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your son Rohit who is 30 is looking for a bride. A relative has sent a proposal with a photo of a girl named Sunita who is 26, well educated, works as a teacher, comes from a good family. You are looking at the photo with your husband in the bedroom. Sunita is dark skinned. Everything else about her is exactly what you were looking for. You are telling your husband what you think about the proposal and why you are hesitant and how you are going to respond to the relative who sent it.
